//1: Dedicated TYPE-CHECK for the scripts/src test tree. The sibling tsconfig.json excludes every test file from typecheck; combined with tsx (which strips types without checking) that let fixtures reference fields the engine types don't have and still run green — the exact 'tests passing on the wrong shape' failure mode. CI runs `typecheck:tests` so every NON-quarantined suite (and every NEW test file — new files are NOT quarantined) is held to the engine's type contract.
//2: moduleDetection=force gives each suite its own module scope (they each declare top-level PASS/FAIL/check helpers that would otherwise collide as globals when the whole tree compiles as one program).
//3: QUARANTINE (the `exclude` list): 25 legacy files carry 143 pre-existing type errors (93 are one mechanical `T[] | undefined` indexing pattern; 10 are genuine wrong-shape fixtures e.g. stateWagesBox16 on W2Fact / interestIncomeBox1). This is a TRACKED, RATCHETING quarantine, NOT a blanket exclusion — the goal is to drive it to []. Fix a file's errors, delete it from this list, keep CI green. Ratchet target + per-file error notes live in docs/todo.md. Do NOT add new files here.
